Hey everyone, I watched this video when it came out, and I have been watching Johnny for a while now, so when this video was played in my political sociology class last week, I was so happy!

We dedicated an entire class period to watching this video while taking notes. It was so tied into our curriculum that the exam featured an essay question about the 5 stages of fascism and how those were achieved in Nazi Germany, directly citing this video.

While watching Johnny's videos, I always think to myself, "everyone needs to know this." I guess I am not the only one who thought that. Apparently, another student sent it to him, when I was thinking of doing that myself lol. He was so amazed by how we were able to explain these very complex systems in a digestible and informative way.

I was reading the threads posted under this video and noticed a lot of you said this is a very scholarly piece of media, and should be played in classes. Well, I am happy to let you all know, that is happening, after only 3 weeks of its release!

I was already a member of Newpress before all of this, so I figured I'd share it here. By the way, I go to Michigan State University. So it was shared with many students.

During and after the bombardment of Kinmen, he and his workers collected these spent shells to recycle them into new weapons.
